items.name: Rucksack (65L+)
items.whyNeeded: SFAS is famous for its ruck marches. You will cover enormous distances.
items.selectionTips: Weight and comfort matter equally. Break in your ruck before arriving. Hip belt fit is critical.

recommendedProducts.name: ALICE Large Field Pack with Frame
recommendedProducts.reason: The proven GI standard for 40+ years. The external aluminum frame is mandatory for SFAS ruck weights — and most candidates use exactly this pack.

items.name: Quality Boots (2 pairs)
items.whyNeeded: Foot health is the #1 factor in making it through SFAS. Blisters can end your selection.
items.selectionTips: Break in both pairs 6+ months before arrival. Know which pair works best for long vs. short distances.

recommendedProducts.name: Danner Ft. Lewis 10" Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Thick full-grain leather outlasts every synthetic alternative. Still the boot of choice for Green Berets — built to handle the Camp Mackall terrain for weeks straight.

items.name: Lensatic Compass + Protractor
items.description: Military lensatic compass and map protractor
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is the primary evaluation in the individual phase. You must navigate precisely.
items.selectionTips: Practice daily for months before the course. SFAS navigation is harder than anything you've done.

recommendedProducts.name: Cammenga 3H Tritium Lensatic Compass
recommendedProducts.reason: The #1 land navigation tool at SFAS. Tritium glow allows accurate azimuth shots in complete darkness — no batteries, no failure, no excuses.

items.name: Knee and Ankle Support
items.whyNeeded: Joint injuries are extremely common. Prevention is essential.
items.selectionTips: Learn to tape ankles and knees yourself. Don't wait for injuries to appear.

items.name: Wool Socks (Quality)
items.description: Merino wool or military wool boot socks
items.whyNeeded: Moisture management and blister prevention. Wool regulates temperature in cold/heat.
items.selectionTips: Darn Tough or Smartwool are worth every penny. Bring at least 10 pairs.

recommendedProducts.name: Darn Tough Boot Full Cushion Sock
recommendedProducts.reason: Lifetime guarantee and merino wool construction. The only sock consistently proven to survive SFAS selection — candidates swear by them.

items.name: Headlamp (Red Filter)
items.whyNeeded: Night navigation requires a reliable light. Red lens preserves night vision.
items.selectionTips: Ensure long battery life. Bring extra batteries (AA preferred).

recommendedProducts.name: Princeton Tec Remix Pro Headlamp
recommendedProducts.reason: Trusted by special operations units for its reliability. Multiple output modes and 300+ hours on standard AA batteries — no recharging needed in the field.

items.name: Leukotape and Moleskin
items.description: High-adhesion athletic tape for blister prevention
items.selectionTips: Leukotape is the gold standard. Apply before blisters form, not after.

recommendedProducts.name: Leukotape P Sports Tape
recommendedProducts.reason: Trusted by physical therapists and SEAL teams alike. Bonds directly to wet skin and stays through days of continuous operations where other tapes peel off.

items.name: Mental Preparation Resources
items.description: Books on mental toughness and SF selection
items.selectionTips: Read 'Lone Survivor' and 'The Operators' for mindset prep. SFAS is 20% physical, 80% mental.

items.name: Electrolyte Supplements
items.selectionTips: Critical for preventing cramping during high-output ruck marches.

recommendedProducts.name: Liquid IV Hydration Multiplier
recommendedProducts.reason: Clinically proven to hydrate 2–3× faster than water alone. SOF candidates use these daily during high-output training to prevent the cramping that ends selections.

items.name: Electronics (Field Operations)
items.description: GPS devices, smartwatches in training areas
items.notes: SFAS tests raw land navigation skill. No GPS assistance allowed.

items.name: Pre-Workout Stimulants
items.notes: Can cause cardiac events under extreme physical stress. Not worth the risk.
